ES1! · CME · PLAN FOR JUN 12, 2026 · BUILT JUN 11 ~18:00 ET

ES Trading Plan — Jun 12 ETH & RTH

Charts: TPO profile + 30-min RTH/ETH + Daily + VX1! (17:52–17:59 ET) · Catalyst: Trump Iran strike cancellation & settlement (12:32–2:39 PM) · MOC: S&P +$809.2M buy imbalance
Last 7,412.75–7,414.50 (+16.75)
Jun 11 RTH: O 7,308 · L 7,262 · H 7,420 · C 7,394.50
POC ZONE 7,294.25–7,307.50 · VAH 7,348.25 · VAL 7,270
Spike base (new) 7,403.50 · single prints 7,387 / 7,337
vs 20-EMA 7,436.25 −23.50
vs 50-EMA 7,298 +114.75 · ATR 105.50
VIX 20.42 ▼ (−0.34%)
▲ What changed today (Jun 11) — and why the whole map re-prices The plan's U1 dip-buy zone worked (open 7,308, morning flush to 7,262 at the VAL/old-spike band, bought), then the 12:32 PM Iran headlines ("cancelled strikes… settlement… signing maybe over the weekend") converted the session into a news-driven trend day up: +158 pts low-to-high, through both fibs, yVAH, and into a spike close at 7,394.50 with a fresh spike base at 7,403.50 and TODAY HIGH 7,420. The rally left TWO single-print ladders below (7,387 and 7,337) — fast, fragile structure. MOC printed a $809M S&P buy imbalance, VIX broke back to 20.42, and ETH is holding above the spike base at ~7,413. Daily reclaimed everything except the 20-EMA 7,436.25, which is now the first overhead test. New chart annotations: daily red 7,532.00, daily cyan moved to 7,341.50, daily red 7,158.75/7,154.25; 30-min fib relabeled 7,396.50; profile labels spike base 7,403.50, single prints 7,387/7,337, POC ZONE box.

01Top-Down Analysis

Reference: last 7,412.75–7,414.50, sitting just above the new spike base 7,403.50, under the daily 20-EMA.

a) Daily — MA stack & drawn levels (updated values)

MAValueDistanceStatus

50-EMA7,298.00+114.75RECLAIMED — now embedded inside the POC ZONE 7,294.25–7,307.50
100-EMA7,143.00+269.75Holding — magnet trade is off the table for now

300-EMA6,786.75+626.00Rising

Horizontal (NEW)Red7,532.00+119.25New daily supply line — clusters with 30m 7,527.50
Horizontal (moved)Cyan7,341.50−71.25Was 7,345.75 — now confluent with VAH 7,348.25 / fib 7,350

Daily narrative: Today printed a high-volume (11.68K) bullish reversal candle — opened 7,397.50 area pre-news framing aside, the RTH story is a 7,262 low bought and a close at 7,412.75, engulfing the prior two down closes. The correction from 7,632.25 now looks like a completed 3-wave −400-pt flush into the 50-EMA zone, with the geopolitical overhang removed intraday. The one technical box left unchecked: price remains below the falling 20-EMA (7,436.25). Reclaiming and holding it converts this from "relief rally" to "uptrend resumed"; rejection there with the spike below is the bears' last clean location.

30-min narrative: The week's pattern is now a completed liquidation → double failed-breakdown → V-recovery sequence. Today's vertical leg cut through every resistance we mapped (fib 7,350, yVAH 7,366.75, cyan 7,383, fib 7,396.50) without a single 30-minute pullback — that's the strength signal, and also the structural weakness: nothing below is tested. ETH is one-time-framing sideways above the spike base, with 7,420/7,424.50 capping so far and the 20-EMA 23 pts overhead.

c) Market Profile — Jun 11 session

MetricValueRead
OPEN / CLOSE7,308.00 / 7,394.50Open in ON value → close +86.5 on a spike
TODAY HIGH / LOW7,420.00 / 7,262.00158-pt range (1.5× ATR)
VAH / POC ZONE / VAL7,348.25 / 7,294.25–7,307.50 / 7,270.00Value still anchored to the MORNING distribution — price closed 46+ above VAH (spike unbacked by value)
yVAH7,366.75Reclaimed and left below — now support reference
Spike base (labeled)7,403.50Late-day spike 7,403.50→7,420 into the close; Dalton spike rules govern tomorrow's open
Single prints (labeled)7,387.00 and 7,337.00Two separate one-TPO ladders from the news rally — speed zones / repair magnets below
Shapeb-base + vertical trend leg (double distribution)Fat morning balance 7,270–7,310, thin elevator above
STRUCTURE MAP (Jun 11 close) 7,436.25 ── daily 20-EMA ◄ the test above 7,424.50 ── 30m pivot 7,420.00 ── TODAY HIGH 7,414.50 ◄◄ YOU ARE HERE (ETH) 7,403.50 ── SPIKE BASE ◄ tonight's line in the sand 7,396.50 ── fib extend / 7,394.50 CLOSE / 7,394 7,387.00 ── SINGLE PRINT ░ speed ░ 7,383.00 ── cyan MA 7,366.75 ── yVAH / 7,366.25 7,350.00 ── fib / 7,348.25 VAH / 7,341.50 daily cyan 7,337.00 ── SINGLE PRINT ░ speed ░ 7,318–22 ── green zone 7,307.50–7,294.25 ── POC ZONE + 50-EMA 7,298 + 7,299.25 7,270.00 ── VAL 7,262.00 ── TODAY LOW 7,258.25 ── old spike base · 7,237.50 lvn · 7,232.25 low

Profile narrative: Classic news-day profile: a balanced b-base built all morning (POC ZONE 7,294.25–7,307.50, right on the 50-EMA), then a vertical leg that never built value. Settlement at 7,394.50 sits 46 pts above VAH with two single-print ladders and zero acceptance between 7,348 and 7,387. Translation: if the spike holds, the market must build value up here tomorrow (bullish continuation); if the spike fails, the repair is fast — single prints fill to 7,387 then potentially 7,337/VAH. The spike base 7,403.50 is the binary reference for every scenario tonight and tomorrow.

d) VIX + macro context

VX1! 20.42 (−0.34%), fading from the 22.6 spike — the entire vol expansion of the past week is unwinding on the Iran de-escalation. Next stop sub-20 re-opens the complacency regime that supported the May melt-up. Macro: Trump cancelled the Iran strikes (12:32 PM), announced a settlement with signing "maybe over the weekend" (Vance attending), Strait reopening on signing, Netanyahu/Gulf leaders briefed. MOC: S&P +$809.2M, NDX +$428.6M, Dow +$892.1M buy imbalances — institutions chased the close. Money-market assets −$21.5B (week to Jun 10) — cash starting to deploy. The asymmetry: the deal is announced but unsigned — "subject to settling over next few days" — so headline risk now cuts BOTH ways into a weekend signing window. A confirmation headline is partly priced; a delay/breakdown headline is not.

04What-If Scenarios — ETH (Jun 11 → Jun 12)

N1 · Spike Acceptance — Balance Above 7,403.50BULL hold★★★★ 28%
Zone / contextETH holds 7,403.50–7,420 all night (current behavior). Asia/Europe ratify the spike.
NarrativeQuiet drift with shallow dips bought at the spike base; pre-RTH probe of 7,420/7,424.50 sets up the 20-EMA test at the open.
SetupLong 7,405–7,408 on spike-base dips · Stop 7,397 (under fib 7,396.50) · T1 7,420 (+13) · T2 7,424.50 (+18) · T3 7,436 (+29) · R:R 1.4→3.2
Confirm / invalidConfirm: 30-min closes keep printing ≥ 7,403.50. Invalid: close < 7,396.50 → N3.
N2 · Overnight Extension — 20-EMA Tag & BeyondBULL momentum★★★ 22%
Zone / contextDeal-progress headlines or Asia chase pushes through 7,420/7,424.50.
Narrative20-EMA 7,436.25 tagged overnight; acceptance there opens 7,448.50 → 7,469.50 (Jun 9 breakdown shelf) — likely the terminal supply for day one.
SetupLong 7,426 on acceptance over 7,424.50 · Stop 7,417 · T1 7,436 (+10) · T2 7,448.50 (+22) · T3 7,469.50 (+43) · R:R 1.1→4.8
Confirm / invalidConfirm: VIX < 20. Invalid: rejection wick at 7,436 + back under 7,424.50 → fade mode.
N3 · Spike Failure — Single-Print Fill to 7,387/7,383BEAR repair (shallow)★★★ 20%
Zone / contextETH loses the spike base 7,403.50 and fib 7,396.50.
NarrativeThe 7,387 single print fills fast (speed zone), cyan 7,383 catches; this is normal post-spike housekeeping, not trend change — unless 7,383 also goes.
SetupShort 7,395 on loss of 7,396.50 · Stop 7,404.75 · T1 7,387 (+8) · T2 7,383 (+12) then assess · R:R 0.8→1.2 (scalp only) — prefer to USE the fill as a long entry at 7,383–7,387, stop 7,375, T 7,403/7,420 (R:R up to 4.6)
Confirm / invalidConfirm: no bid at 7,400 retest. Invalid for the dip-long: 30-min close < 7,383 → N4.
N4 · Deep Repair — yVAH then VAH/Fib ClusterBEAR rotation★★ 13%
Zone / context7,383 fails; the whole afternoon leg unwinds toward value.
NarrativeyVAH 7,366.75 first response; below it the 7,341.50–7,350 cluster (VAH + fib + daily cyan) is the terminal repair zone and a major dip-buy location ahead of Friday RTH.
SetupResponsive long 7,343–7,349 at the cluster · Stop 7,334 (under single print 7,337) · T1 7,366 (+19) · T2 7,387 (+40) · T3 7,403 (+56) · R:R 2.1→6.2
Confirm / invalidConfirm: VIX still ≤ 21 (orderly repair). Invalid: 30-min close < 7,337 → N5 regime.
N5 · Headline Reversal — Deal WobbleBEAR tail★ 9%
Zone / context"Settlement subject to settling" breaks down — Iran/Israel re-escalation headline overnight.
NarrativeBoth single prints fill; price air-pockets through 7,337 → green zone 7,318 → POC ZONE 7,294–7,307 (50-EMA), today's entire rally unwound.
SetupNo fresh shorts into the hole. Pre-stage responsive longs 7,296–7,304 (POC ZONE + 50-EMA) · Stop 7,288 · T1 7,318 · T2 7,337 · R:R 1.8→4.5
Confirm / invalidConfirm: headline-driven, VIX > 22. Invalid: POC ZONE fails → 7,270/7,262 and stand down.
N6 · Signing-Confirmed Melt — Gap Toward 7,448+BULL outlier★ 8%
Zone / contextConcrete signing date/details hit overnight; VIX breaks 19.
NarrativeOne-time-frame up through 7,436/7,448.50; 7,469.50–7,491.50 shelf reached before Friday's open.
SetupDon't chase; buy the first 30-min pullback that holds 7,436 from above · Stop 7,426 · T1 7,448.50 · T2 7,469.50 · R:R 1.3→3.4
Confirm / invalidConfirm: volume expansion on the headline. Invalid: back under 7,424.50.

05Probability Matrix — ETH

RankScenarioDirectionProb★
1N1 Spike acceptance balance 7,403–7,420Bull28%★★★★
2N2 Extension to 20-EMA 7,436+Bull22%★★★
3N3 Spike failure → 7,387/7,383 fillBear-shallow20%★★★
4N4 Deep repair → 7,341.50–7,350Bear-rotation13%★★
5N5 Headline reversal → POC ZONEBear tail9%★
6N6 Signing melt → 7,448/7,469Bull outlier8%★
CombinedBull 58%Bear 42%
Base case (ETH)Spike acceptance: balance 7,400–7,424 overnight with dips into the 7,396.50–7,403.50 band bought, setting up a 20-EMA (7,436.25) test in Friday RTH. Reasoning: the spike is news-backed (not just short-covering), MOC showed real institutional demand, VIX is unwinding, and ETH is already holding above the base. The counterweight — 105+ pts of extension above value on an unsigned deal — argues for buying repairs, not chasing highs.

06Decision Map — ETH

ETH @ 7,413 (spike base 7,403.50 · 20-EMA 7,436.25 overhead)
│
├─► HOLDS ≥ 7,403.50 (acceptance — current state)
│     ├─ balance 7,403–7,420 → N1 → Friday opens with 20-EMA test pending
│     └─ breaks 7,424.50 → tag 20-EMA 7,436
│            ├─ rejected → fade back to 7,420/7,406
│            └─ accepted → N2/N6 → 7,448.50 → 7,469.50
│
└─► LOSES 7,403.50 → tests fib 7,396.50 / close 7,394.50
      ├─ holds 7,394–96 → bull intact, rotation only
      └─ closes < 7,396.50 → N3 → single print fills → 7,387 → cyan 7,383
            ├─ 7,383–87 BOUGHT (preferred long) → back to 7,403/7,420

Position size vs ATR — applies to all threeATR 105.5, headline tape, unsigned deal: 60% size on momentum entries, 75% on repair-cluster entries. No shorts as primary trades tonight — the only short is the N3 scalp, and the fill is better used as a long entry. Hard rule: any Iran re-escalation headline = flat first, read later.

09Structural Warnings

⚠ TWO single-print ladders below: 7,387 and 7,337Today's rally built no value between 7,348 and 7,387. Both ladders are speed zones — repairs through them will be violent and fast. Never place passive stops mid-ladder; structure your risk at 7,396.50 / 7,383 / 7,334 instead.
⚠ Spike base 7,403.50 — Dalton open rules tomorrowOpen ABOVE 7,420 = spike accepted, higher prices being ratified (buy dips to the base). Open WITHIN 7,394.50–7,420 = balancing the spike (two-sided). Open BELOW 7,394.50 = spike rejected — the single prints fill and 7,366/7,348 come fast. This single reference sets Friday's template.
⚠ Extension from value: +105 to +119 pts above the POC ZONESettlement closed 46 pts above its own VAH. Until value migrates up, chasing above 7,436 is buying maximum extension — let the 20-EMA or a repair give you location.
⚠ Unsigned deal = binary weekend risk"Settlement subject to settling over the next few days… signing maybe over the weekend." A confirmation is partially priced; a delay/breakdown is not. Friday afternoon positioning ahead of the weekend can cut either way — expect de-risking flows after 2 PM if no signing date is confirmed.
ℹ 20-EMA 7,436.25 — first retest after a 4-day breakStatistically the first tag of a lost-and-falling 20-EMA rejects. Acceptance above it (two 30-min closes) is the signal that the correction is over and 7,448/7,469/7,491 unlock.
ℹ VIX 20.42 — regime gateSub-20 re-opens the melt-up regime; back above 22 invalidates the bull template regardless of price.

10Session Transition — ETH → Friday RTH open

If price at zone into RTH openImplicationLikely day type
> 7,436 (above 20-EMA)Spike + MA reclaimed overnightTrend-up / gap-and-go toward 7,448–7,469
7,420–7,436Spike accepted, 20-EMA decision at the bellNormal-variation up; buy dips to 7,403–7,410
7,394.50–7,420 (in spike)Balancing the spike — two-sidedRotational; trade the 7,403.50 polarity
7,366–7,394.50 (below spike)Spike rejected; single print 7,387 filledRepair day toward 7,348–7,366; b-shape if yVAH holds
7,337–7,366Deep repair into the VAH/fib clusterResponsive-buy day from 7,341.50–7,350; afternoon recovery attempt
< 7,337Both ladders filled — news has turnedTrend-down to POC ZONE 7,294–7,307 / 7,270–7,262

11Final Summary — ETH

ItemCall
Macro biasBULL — full MA stack rising below price; geopolitical overhang lifting; cash deploying
Intermediate biasBULL / repairing — correction likely terminal at 7,232; only the 20-EMA left to reclaim
Overnight biasBULL — buy dips at structure, never chase
Base caseSpike acceptance: balance 7,400–7,424, dips into 7,396.50–7,403.50 bought, 20-EMA test pending Friday
Primary tradeLong 7,405–7,408 (spike base), stop 7,397, targets 7,420 / 7,424.50 / 7,436
Best-location alternateLong 7,383–7,387 on a single-print fill, stop 7,375 → 7,396.50 / 7,403.50 / 7,420
Key bull level7,424.50 → 20-EMA 7,436.25 (acceptance = correction over, 7,448/7,469 unlock)
Key bear level7,396.50 (spike fails) → 7,383 → 7,366.75 → 7,341.50–7,350 terminal repair
Biggest riskHeadline whipsaw on an unsigned deal — air pockets through the single-print ladders in either direction
Night-type expectationBalance above spike (≈50%) · extension to 7,436+ (≈30%) · repair below 7,396.50 (≈20%)

01Top-Down — Friday Jun 12 RTH framing

Same chart evidence as the ETH tab (full tables there). Re-cast for the day session. Verify overnight extremes at the bell and shift the spike-rule template accordingly.

Daily: Yesterday's reversal candle closed above the 50/100/200/300-EMA stack with only the falling 20-EMA 7,436.25 overhead (−23.5 from last). A close above it Friday would complete the V-repair and re-target 7,491 → 7,527.50/7,532 → ATH 7,632.25 next week. ATR 105.50.

30-min: One-time-frame up from 7,262 with zero tested structure between 7,348 and 7,387. Working ladder above: 7,420 / 7,424.50 / 7,436 / 7,448.50 / 7,469.50 / 7,491.50. Below: spike base 7,403.50, fib 7,396.50, single print 7,387, cyan 7,383, yVAH 7,366.75, VAH/fib/daily-cyan 7,341.50–7,350, single print 7,337, green zone 7,318–22, POC ZONE 7,294.25–7,307.50 (+50-EMA).

Profile: Jun 11 = b-base + news trend leg + spike close. Friday's tell is where value builds: overlapping-to-higher value above 7,390 ratifies the move; value rebuilding below 7,366 says the spike was excess and the repair is on. The Dalton spike rules vs 7,403.50 set the opening template (see Warnings).

VIX/macro: VX1 20.42 and falling = confirmation. Friday-specific: weekend signing window means afternoon flows can de-risk if no concrete signing confirmation prints by ~2 PM — watch MOC direction for the tell (yesterday: +$809M SPX buy).

02Key Levels — RTH working ladder

Master table on the ETH tab. RTH-critical subset (ref 7,413; re-anchor to ON extremes pre-open):
ZonePriceWhy it matters Friday★
R57,491.50 / 7,508.25Jun-9 swing high — stretch target on a signing headline★★★
R47,448.50 / 7,469.50Breakdown shelf — first real supply above the 20-EMA★★★★
R3 🔗7,424.50–7,436.25Pivot + 20-EMA — THE decision zone of the day★★★★★
R27,420.00Yesterday's high / spike top★★★
PIVOT7,394.50–7,403.50Spike base + fib 7,396.50 + close — polarity band★★★★★

03Context Into the Friday Open

DimensionReadingImplication
Prior day typeNews trend-day up with spike closeSpike rules govern the open; trend days are typically followed by acceptance-or-repair, rarely immediate reversal
Open vs spike>7,420 accept · 7,394.50–7,420 balance · <7,394.50 rejectThe first 30 minutes vs 7,403.50 sets the template
Value placementYesterday's value 7,270–7,348 — far below priceFriday must BUILD value 7,380+ to ratify; watch the developing VA from the open
InventoryLongs from 7,262–7,310 massively profitable; ON holders long from 7,403+Profit-taking dips are normal and buyable at structure — not trend changes
Day-of-week / eventFriday + weekend signing windowTwo-session day: directional morning, positioning afternoon; reduce size after 2 PM
ATR budget105.5 — full-bull day ≈ 7,470–7,500; full-bear ≈ 7,300–7,330Both extremes map exactly onto the R4 shelf and the POC ZONE

04What-If Scenarios — RTH Friday Jun 12

F1 · Spike-Accepted Open → 20-EMA Battle → Grind HigherBULL base case★★★★ 30%
Open / contextOpens 7,400–7,430 (in/above spike). Early dip to 7,396–7,406 bought, then the 20-EMA 7,436.25 fight.
NarrativeValue builds 7,390–7,430 (ratification); afternoon acceptance over the 20-EMA tags 7,448.50; P-shape close if a signing date prints.
SetupLong 7,398–7,406 (spike base zone) · Stop 7,390 · T1 7,420 (+16) · T2 7,436 (+32) · T3 7,448.50 (+45) · R:R 1.6→4.5
Confirm / invalidConfirm: first 30-min holds > 7,394.50. Invalid: 30-min close below 7,394.50 → F3.
F2 · Gap-and-Go on Deal ProgressBULL trend★★★ 18%
Open / contextOpens > 7,436 on a signing-confirmation headline; VIX < 20.
Narrative20-EMA reclaimed at the bell; one-time-frame up through 7,448.50 to the 7,469.50–7,491.50 shelf; weekly close near the highs.
SetupLong first pullback holding 7,436 from above · Stop 7,426 · T1 7,448.50 · T2 7,469.50 · T3 7,491.50 · R:R 1.3→5.6
Confirm / invalidConfirm: gap holds first 30 min. Invalid: re-entry below 7,424.50 = gap-fill risk to 7,403.
F3 · Spike Rejected → Single-Print Repair → Responsive BuyREPAIR then BULL★★★ 22%
Open / contextOpens/breaks below 7,394.50 — spike rejected; profit-taking dominates the morning.
Narrative7,387 fills instantly, cyan 7,383 then yVAH 7,366.75 tested; buyers respond at 7,366 or at the 7,341.50–7,350 cluster; afternoon recovery toward the spike base.
Setup(a) Scalp short 7,392 → 7,383/7,367 (tight, stop 7,400). (b) Core trade: long 7,343–7,350 or 7,365–7,368 on responsive turn · Stop 7,334/7,357 · T1 7,387 · T2 7,403 · R:R up to 5.0
Confirm / invalidConfirm: orderly repair, VIX ≤ 21. Invalid: close < 7,337 → F5.
F4 · 20-EMA Hard Rejection → Range Day in the SpikeNEUTRAL fade★★ 15%
Open / contextMorning tags 7,430–7,438, rejects; but 7,394–7,403 holds every test below.
NarrativeFriday bracket 7,394–7,436: fade both edges, value overlaps higher (still constructive for next week).
SetupShort 7,432–7,436 stop 7,443 → T 7,415/7,404; Long 7,397–7,403 stop 7,390 → T 7,420/7,430 · R:R 1.5→3.5 per rotation
Confirm / invalidConfirm: two failed edge-breaks by midday. Invalid: 30-min close outside 7,394–7,436.
F5 · Deal-Wobble Liquidation → POC ZONEBEAR tail★★ 10%
Open / contextNegative Iran headline (delay/re-escalation); 7,337 single print fails.
NarrativeFull unwind of the news leg: 7,318 → POC ZONE 7,294.25–7,307.50 (50-EMA), worst case VAL 7,270 / yLow 7,262. Weekend de-risking amplifies.
SetupShort 7,334 on loss of 7,337 · Stop 7,344 · T1 7,318 (+16) · T2 7,305 (+29) · T3 7,294 (+40) · R:R 1.6→4.0. Then responsive long 7,296–7,304 stop 7,288 if VIX < 23.
Confirm / invalidConfirm: headline-driven volume spike. Invalid: reclaim 7,348.
F6 · Friday-Afternoon De-Risk FadeLATE-DAY pattern★ 5%
Open / contextConstructive morning, but by 2 PM no signing date confirmed.
NarrativeWeekend-risk premium gets bought (VIX bid), longs trim; drift from the highs back to the spike base into MOC. Not a reversal — a positioning fade.
SetupTrim all longs by 2 PM if unconfirmed; optional scalp short from 7,430s to 7,410/7,404 · stop above HOD · R:R ~2.0
Confirm / invalidConfirm: VIX ticking up while ES flat. Invalid: signing confirmed → stand down, F2 resumes.

05Probability Matrix — RTH

RankScenarioDirectionProb★
1F1 Spike accepted → 20-EMA battle → grind upBull30%★★★★
2F3 Spike rejected → repair → responsive buyRepair→Bull22%★★★
3F2 Gap-and-go > 20-EMABull18%★★★
4F4 20-EMA rejection → spike-range dayNeutral15%★★
5F5 Deal-wobble liquidation → POC ZONEBear10%★★
6F6 Friday de-risk fade (overlay)Late-day5%★
CombinedBull/repair 70%Bear 15%Neutral 15%
Base case (RTH)Spike-accepted open, early dip into 7,396–7,406 bought, a real fight at the 20-EMA 7,436.25, and value building 7,390–7,430 to ratify yesterday's move. Reasoning: trend days with institutional MOC buying and falling VIX get follow-through far more often than reversal; the repair scenarios (F3) end at buyable structure, not trend change. The one true bear path requires a negative headline — price alone doesn't get there.

06Decision Map — RTH

FRIDAY RTH OPEN vs SPIKE (base 7,403.50 · close 7,394.50 · high 7,420)
│
├─► OPEN > 7,420 (spike accepted strongly)
│     ├─ holds 7,420 retest → F1/F2 → 20-EMA 7,436 → 7,448.50 → 7,469
│     └─ fails back into spike → balance 7,394–7,436 (F4)
│
├─► OPEN 7,394.50–7,420 (balancing — most likely)
│     ├─ dip 7,396–7,406 BOUGHT → F1 long → 7,420 → 20-EMA 7,436
│     │      ├─ accepted → 7,448.50 → 7,469.50
│     │      └─ rejected ×2 → F4 range fade edges 7,397↔7,436
│     └─ 30-min close < 7,394.50 → spike rejected → F3
│            └─ 7,387 fills → 7,383 → 7,366.75 → BUY 7,341.50–7,350
│                   └─ 7,337 fails (headline) → F5 → 7,318 → POC ZONE 7,294–7,307
│
└─► OPEN < 7,394.50 (spike rejected at the bell)
      ├─ look-below-and-fail, reclaims 7,403.50 → strong long → 7,420+
      └─ acceptance lower → F3 repair path above; afternoon recovery attempt
      
TIME RULE: after 2 PM with NO signing confirmation → trim, F6 overlay active
LevelHoldsBreaks
7,436.25 20-EMARange day cap (F4)Correction officially over → 7,448 → 7,469 → 7,491
7,420Spike-top resistance on first test20-EMA test immediate
7,394.50–7,403.50Bull template intact — buy dipsSingle-print repair → 7,387/7,383
7,366.75 yVAHShallow-repair turn — long7,341.50–7,350 cluster next
7,341.50–7,350Terminal repair — core long7,337 print fails → headline regime → POC ZONE
7,294.25–7,307.5050-EMA defense → rally rebuilds next week7,270/7,262 → bull thesis suspended

07Bias Flip Conditions — RTH

Starting biasFlip toTriggerAction
Bull (default)Trend-up confirmTwo 30-min closes > 7,436.25Hold runners 7,448/7,469; trail under 7,424.50
BullNeutral / repair30-min close < 7,394.50Flatten; bid 7,383–7,387 then 7,341.50–7,350
RepairBull restoredReclaim 7,403.50Resume buy-dips template, targets 7,420/7,436
RepairBear30-min close < 7,337 (esp. on headline)F5 short to 7,318/POC ZONE; no longs until 7,294–7,307
BearStabilizingPOC ZONE holds + VIX < 23Responsive long 7,296–7,304, half size
Any longDe-risk2 PM, no signing confirmation, VIX uptickingF6: trim to core, tighten all trails

08Tactical Playbook — RTH top 3

Click a trade to expand. Ranked by edge for Friday's day session.
#1
Spike-Base Dip Buy → 20-EMA (F1)
LONGup to 4.5R▶
Entry
Long 7,398–7,406 on the first profit-taking dip into the spike-base band (fib 7,396.50 / base 7,403.50)
Stop
7,390 — below close 7,394.50 · risk 8–16 pts
Targets
T1 7,420 (+16)T2 7,436 20-EMA (+32)T3 7,448.50 (+45)
Scaling
½ at T1 · ¼ at T2 (take it on first tag — rejection odds high) · runner only on 20-EMA acceptance
R:R
1.6R / 3.2R / 4.5R
Kill switch
30-min close < 7,394.50 → out, re-bid lower per #2
#2
Terminal-Repair Cluster Long 7,341.50–7,350 (F3 core)
LONGup to 5.0R▶
Entry
Long 7,343–7,350 at VAH 7,348.25 + fib 7,350 + daily cyan 7,341.50 after a spike-rejection repair (or 7,365–68 at yVAH if the turn comes early)
Stop
7,334 — under single print 7,337 · risk 9–16 pts
Targets
T1 7,366.75 (+17)T2 7,387 (+37)T3 7,403.50 (+54)
Scaling
½ at T1 · ¼ at T2 · runner to the spike base; add-back signal = reclaim of 7,403.50
R:R
1.7R / 3.4R / 5.0R
Kill switch
30-min close < 7,337 or any re-escalation headline → flat, wait for POC ZONE
#3
20-EMA Acceptance Continuation (F2)
LONGup to 5.6R▶
Entry
Long 7,437–7,440 ONLY after two 30-min closes above 7,436.25, on the first pullback that holds it from above
Stop
7,426 — back under 7,424.50 pivot · risk 11–14 pts
Targets
T1 7,448.50 (+10)T2 7,469.50 (+31)T3 7,491.50 (+53)
Scaling
⅓ at T1 · ⅓ at T2 · runner to the shelf, trail under each 30-min higher low; trim hard by 2 PM if no signing date
R:R
0.9R / 2.6R / 5.6R
Kill switch
Re-entry below 7,424.50 → acceptance failed, exit
Position size vs ATR — applies to all threeATR 105.5 on a headline Friday: 60–70% size, ¼-size only after 2 PM without a signing confirmation. Shorts are scalps (F3 fill, F4 edge, F6 fade) — never core positions while price is above 7,337 and VIX is below 22.

09Structural Warnings — RTH

⚠ Spike rules at the open (vs base 7,403.50)Open above 7,420 = acceptance (buy dips). Open 7,394.50–7,420 = balancing (trade 7,403.50 polarity). Open below 7,394.50 = rejection (single prints fill — don't fight the repair, buy its completion at 7,366 or 7,341.50–7,350).
⚠ Untested air: 7,348 → 7,387The rally built no value here. Any RTH revisit moves fast — entries at the labeled edges only (7,387 / 7,383 / 7,366.75 / 7,350), never mid-air.
⚠ Headline binary into the weekendDeal announced, unsigned, "maybe over the weekend." Holding full-size longs over the weekend is a news bet, not a trade. F6 time rule: trim by 2 PM without confirmation.
⚠ First 20-EMA retest after a 4-day break7,436.25 with 7,424.50 in front: expect a fight, take T2 profits on first tag, only trust acceptance (2× 30-min closes).
⚠ Extension above valueClose was +46 over its own VAH, +105 over the POC ZONE. If value doesn't migrate up by midday (developing VA stuck below 7,380), the afternoon leans F4/F6 — tighten longs.
ℹ VIX gate< 20 = melt regime (favor F1/F2) · 20–22 = two-sided · > 22 = bull template suspended.

10Session Transition — Friday close → next week

If Friday closes at…ImplicationNext-week lean
> 7,448.5020-EMA reclaimed AND shelf brokenBull continuation: 7,469 → 7,491 → 7,527.50/7,532; ATH retest in play on signing
7,436–7,448Correction over, momentum buildingBuy-dips regime; 7,403.50 = weekly pivot
7,394.50–7,436 (in spike)Ratified but capped — weekend wait modeSigning = gap up; delay = retest 7,366/7,348 first
7,337–7,394.50Spike rejected, repair done into the closeTwo-sided; 7,341.50–7,350 must hold Monday
< 7,337News leg fully unwoundBack to the POC ZONE auction 7,294–7,307; bear template re-armed below 7,294

11Final Summary — RTH Friday Jun 12

ItemCall
Macro biasBULL — geopolitical catalyst resolved (pending signature), MA stack rising, cash deploying
Intermediate biasBULL / repairing — only the 20-EMA 7,436.25 left; acceptance there ends the correction
Intraday biasBULL — buy structured dips; shorts are scalps only
Base caseSpike-accepted open → dip to 7,396–7,406 bought → 20-EMA battle → value ratifies 7,390–7,430
Primary tradeLong 7,398–7,406, stop 7,390, targets 7,420 / 7,436 / 7,448.50 (F1)
Best-location alternateLong 7,343–7,350 on a spike-rejection repair, stop 7,334 → 7,366.75 / 7,387 / 7,403.50 (F3)
Key bull level7,436.25 (20-EMA) — acceptance unlocks 7,448.50 / 7,469.50 / 7,491.50
Key bear level7,337 (lower single print) — close below on a headline re-opens 7,318 → POC ZONE 7,294–7,307
Biggest riskWeekend headline binary: unsigned deal whipsaw through untested air in either direction; 2 PM de-risk rule applies
Day-type expectationNormal-variation up / P-shape (≈45%) · repair-then-recover (≈22%) · trend-up (≈18%) · range (≈10%) · headline trend-down (≈5%)
ES Plan for Jun 12, 2026 (ETH + RTH) · built Jun 11 ~18:00 ET from TPO + 30-min + Daily + VX1! charts · Last 7,413 · Jun 11: O 7,308 / L 7,262 / H 7,420 / C 7,394.50 · Spike base 7,403.50 · Catalyst: Iran settlement (unsigned) · MOC +$809M SPX · Not financial advice. Re-anchor to overnight extremes pre-open.
